summary: Studio‑like soundboard recording captures an excellent 1976 Boston run with standout St. Stephen > Dancing > Music Never Stopped and Eyes > Stella Blue sequences
review: The three reviewers rave about the fluid, precise performance that sounds almost like a studio album, noting superb soundboard quality with no audible cuts. Highlights include a “out‑of‑this‑world” St. Stephen > Dancing > Music Never Stopped suite, a soaring Eyes > Stella Blue pairing, and strong first‑set rockers such as Lazy Lightning and Ship of Fools. Donna Jean’s vocals also receive special praise.

ai_rating.rationale: All three 5‑star reviews describe an exceptional performance and a pristine, studio‑like soundboard recording; the consistency and detail give high confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crystal‑clear, studio‑like, with no audio cuts (unlike other copies). Fluid mix, precise balance; no reported dropouts or muffiness.

standout_songs: St. Stephen > Dancing > Music Never Stopped
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a strong, well‑balanced set that blends rockers and ballads, with deep, flowing sequences that keep the momentum high throughout both sets.
